segments.s761-g41: برین گونه = بر + این + گونه is an adverbial phrase 'بر این گونه' meaning 'in this way' placed at the clause start. تا introduces the temporal limit 'until' and governs the following clause. سال شد is literally 'the years became' (شد, past of شدن) — an idiom meaning 'he became (so many) years old' with an implicit subject; بر دو هشت is a numeric complement where بر marks attainment ('to/amounting to') and دو هشت (literally 'two eights') is an archaic way of stating 16.
segments.s761-g42: همی is a classical adverb meaning 'kept on/continually' that scopes over the action. ماه و خورشید ('moon and sun') is a poetic pairing referring to the recurring lunar and solar cycles (i.e., months/years). بر سر گذشت is composed of the preposition بر ('on/upon') + سر ('head') + گذشت (past of گذشتن 'to pass'), forming the idiom 'passed over the head' meaning time passed above/over him.
segments.s761-g33: The clause is SVO with the verb 'دهم' (I give) omitted in the words list but understood in the full sentence; here 'بزرگی' (object) precedes the verb in poetic word order. 'هر که کهتر بود' is a relative clause (هر که = 'whoever') with 'کهتر' described by the past-tense copula 'بود' ('was'), so literally 'I give greatness to whoever was lesser.'
segments.s761-g34: This clause contains a negated verb phrase: the surface 'نیازارم' should be read as negative + verb (effectively 'I do not harm/afflict'); 'آن' is a demonstrative 'that/that one'; 'راکه' is a relative connector 'that/which'; the final 'مهتر بود' is another relative clause with 'مهتر' ('the greater/elder') and past copula 'بود'. The whole yields 'I do not afflict that one who was greater.'
segments.s761-g35: 'نجویم' is first-person present (here with an initial negative particle in the surface form), so the clause negates the seeking: 'I (do not) seek.' The direct objects 'بزرگی و فرزانگی' follow, meaning 'greatness and wisdom'; note the verb 'seek' governs both nouns connected by 'و.'
segments.s761-g36: 'همان' (= 'the same' / 'that very') points back to what the speaker actually values; a short nominal series follows with nouns joined by 'و'. The list 'رزم و تندی و مردانگی' should be read as the preferred qualities: 'battle, fierceness, and manliness.'
segments.s761-g37: The clause begins with the relative/subordinating 'که' ('that/so that'); 'برکس نماند' is an idiomatic poetic phrasing meaning 'remain for anyone' (برکس = 'on any person'), with 'نماند' (does not remain / is not left) in present/past-interpretive tense. 'همی' is an emphatic adverb ('ever/at all'), and 'زور و بخت' are coordinated nouns 'strength and fortune.'
segments.s761-g38: A negative enumerative series introduced by repeated 'نه' ('neither... nor...') lists the things that do not remain: 'گنج' (treasure), 'دیهیم شاهی' (literally 'rural/regional kingship' — an adjectival 'دیهیم' qualifying 'شاهی'), and 'تخت' (throne). The nouns are coordinated and the negation is distributive.
segments.s761-g39: 'همی' is emphatic here ('ever/always'); the phrase 'نام جاوید باید' uses 'باید' (must/ought) with the nominal predicate 'نام جاوید' ('an everlasting name' or 'eternal renown'), and 'نه کام' provides contrast by negation ('not desire'), so the clause reads 'What is needed is an eternal name, not desire.'
segments.s761-g40: 'بینداز' is an imperative/hortative verb 'cast away/throw aside' governing 'کام' (desire); then 'و' links to the second imperative 'برافراز' ('raise up/exalt') with object 'نام' (name/reputation). Together: 'Cast away desire and exalt the name.'
segments.s761-g1: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when' introducing a temporal clause; بگذشت is a past tense verb 'passed (away/away from life or from the scene)' and ز + و (here written as ز و) functions as 'from him/after him' (classical fused form). شاه شد is a simple past copular construction 'became king' (شد = become) with the subject name following in the next phrase.
segments.s761-g2: به ماه ... روز ... gives the calendrical specification: به + ماه names the month ('in the month of'), سفندار مذ is the (compound) month-name Spandarmad, and روز ارد names the day within that month; word order is prepositional phrase (به ماه) + month-name + appositionally the day.
segments.s761-g3: چه گفت is an interrogative 'what said' (literary word order: interrogative + verb), followed by the definite demonstrative آن 'that' and the noun phrase سخنگوی مرد دلیر 'the eloquent/horseman-like brave man' in apposition; the whole segment is the question-stem 'What did that speaker, the brave man, say?'.
segments.s761-g4: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when'; از گردش روز 'from the turning/circuit of the day' is a genitival phrase indicating 'at the end of the day's course'; برگشت is the past 'returned', and سیر (here adjectival/nominal) means 'sated/full' describing his state on return.
segments.s761-g5: که opens a subordinate/explanatory clause ('that' / 'for that'); باری is an adverb 'once, formerly'; نزادی is an archaic past-form of the root زادن 'to beget/give birth' (here a past action concerning birth); مرا is the accusative 'me' (object), and مادرم 'my mother' identifies the agent — the phrase reports the birth-related fact in a compressed, archaic order typical of epic diction.
segments.s761-g6: نگشتی is a past form of گشتن used here with the sense 'turn/withdraw' (classical negative-looking form but functionally 'did not turn/was not turned' in context); سپهر بلند is the nominative subject 'the lofty sky/heaven' and از برم ('از' + بر + ـم) marks direction 'from my side / away from me', so the clause states that the high heaven did not turn away from the speaker.
segments.s761-g7: به + noun phrase marks a locative/figurative location 'in/within': پرگار تنگ 'a narrow compass' (پرگار = compass/circumference used metaphorically) and میان دو گوی 'between two spheres/orbs' (گوی = orb/sphere). The whole segment sets the spatial/metaphorical frame.
segments.s761-g8: چه گویم is a rhetorical question 'what shall I say' with گویم in the present/subjunctive-type form 'I may say / I should say'; جز از خامشی نیست روی is a compressed negation: جز ... نیست 'there is nothing except ...' + خامشی 'silence' as the only thing, and روی (here 'face/manifestation') emphasizes there is no other expression — thus: 'What can I say, except that nothing remains but silence.'
segments.s761-g9: The correlative negation 'نه ... نه ...' = 'neither ... nor ...' links two noun phrases; 'روز بزرگی' = 'a day of greatness' (noun + genitive/qualifying noun) and the repeated 'روز نیاز' = 'a day of need'. Word order is noun then qualifier, a common Persian noun phrase order.
segments.s761-g10: 'نماند' (ن + ماند) is a negative/present-oriented verbal form meaning 'does not remain / is not left'; 'همی' is an older continuous/emphatic particle meaning 'always / for long' (here reinforcing duration); 'برکسی' is the fused preposition+indefinite ('بر' + 'کسی') meaning 'for anyone' (written together in the line); 'بر دراز' literally 'on/for long' — together: 'does not remain long for anyone.'
segments.s761-g11: 'زمانه' here means the state of affairs / fortune ('the times'); 'ز ما نیست' literally 'is not from/with us' = 'is not on our side'; 'چون بنگری' is a subordinating temporal clause 'when/if you look' — 'چون' used in an older/poetic sense introducing 'when', and 'بنگری' is the second-person present/subjunctive form of 'نگاه کردن' (you look/if you look).
segments.s761-g12: The verb 'ندارد' (does not have) precedes the indefinite subject for emphasis; 'کسی' = 'anyone' and 'آلت داوری' = 'the instrument/means of judgment' (a compound where 'آلت' = 'instrument/means' qualifies 'داوری' = 'judgment'). The clause states there is no one who possesses the faculty/means of judging.
segments.s761-g13: 'به' introduces a relation 'with regard to / for'; 'یارای خوان' = 'prowess/ability for the table' (ability + noun 'خوان' = banquet); 'و' links the two phrases; 'به پیمای جام' = 'and with regard to the measure/measure‑capacity of the cup' (پیمای here is the construct/qualifying form from پیمانه/پیمود meaning 'measure' used poetically). Both prepositional phrases set the domains (feasting/drinking) relevant to the following imperative.
segments.s761-g14: 'ز تیمار گیتی' = 'because of / from the world's care' (ز = from/because of, تیمار = care/solicitude, گیتی = the world); 'مبر' is an archaic negative imperative 'do not take / do not let be taken'; 'هیچ نام' = 'any name', where 'نام' here means 'fame/reputation'. Together the clause is an admonition: do not allow worldly worries to strip away your reputation in the matters just named.
segments.s761-g15: 'اگر' = 'if' introduces the conditional; 'چرخ گردان' is a poetic personification of fate/fortune ('the revolving wheel'); 'کشد' is the main verb 'draws/pulls' (here transitive: 'pulls you'); 'زین تو' = 'from your saddle / off your mount' (زین = saddle, followed by the possessive 'تو'). Note: in the provided senses list 'کشد' did not include the pulling sense, so that sense is the intended one here.
segments.s761-g16: 'سرانجام' = 'in the end / finally' (adverbial); the verbless clause 'خاکست بالین تو' is a poetic nominal predicate where 'خاکست' = 'ashes' and 'بالین تو' = 'your pillow' (possessive follows). Persian often omits the copula; the meaning is 'your pillow will be ashes' (i.e., you will lie on ashes in the end).
segments.s761-g25: به ژرفی نگه کن is an imperative: کن (do) takes نگه (a noun 'gaze/watch') as its object and the prepositional phrase به ژرفی functions adverbially ('deeply/closely'). که begins a subordinate clause ('that/what...') and با یزدگرد marks the patient/target with با ('with/against') rather than indicating accompaniment here (i.e. 'to/at Yazdegerd'). Word order places the imperative phrase before the clause it introduces.
segments.s761-g26: چه کرد is an exclamatory/interrogative formula: چه ('what') + کرد (past of 'to do') asking/declaring 'what (was) done'. این برافراخته هفت گرد is the subject: این (this) + برافراخته (lofty/proud) + هفت گرد (proper name). Note the verb (کرد) precedes the subject here, a common poetic inversion for emphasis.
segments.s761-g27: چو ... بنشست is a temporal clause: چو = 'when' and بنشست is the past tense 'he sat down.' بر خسروی گاه is a locative phrase 'on/at the kingly seat' (بر + خسروی + گاه), where خسروی functions as 'king/royal' and گاه as 'seat/occasion/time.' شاد is a predicative/adverbial adjective ('happy/joyful') modifying the sitter; its placement after the verb is typical in poetry.
segments.s761-g28: کلاه بزرگی is the direct object: کلاه ('hat') modified by بزرگی ('greatness/size' used adjectivally = 'large'). به سر is a locative/directional phrase ('onto the head') and برنهاد is the past tense verb 'he placed/put on.' The sequence shows object + locative + verb, a normal poetic order.
segments.s761-g29: چنین گفت is a formula 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus', گفت = verbal past 'said'). کز introduces a dependent clause meaning 'that/from which' and governs the phrase دور چرخ روان: دور = 'remote/distant', چرخ = 'wheel/sky/fate' (here poetic 'wheel of heaven/fate'), and روان modifies چرخ as 'moving/spinning' ('the distant moving wheel').
segments.s761-g30: منم is the emphatic copula 'I am' (من + هستم contracted). پاک modifies فرزند ('pure child/offspring'); نوشین روان is a compound epithet: نوشین ('sweet/delightful') qualifies روان ('soul/spirit'), giving 'sweet‑souled.' Together the noun phrase declares his lineage/quality: 'I am a pure child of sweet‑souled [stock].'
segments.s761-g31: پدر بر پدر is a genitival/successive phrase meaning 'from father to father' (i.e. by hereditary succession). پادشاهی is the noun 'kingship/kingdom' and مراست functions as 'is mine' (literally مرا + است contracted), so the clause states that kingship, passed down father to father, belongs to him.
segments.s761-g32: This is a coordinative list of possessions: خور ('sun'), خوشه ('ear/cluster' here 'ear (of grain)') and برج ماهی ('the zodiac sign of the Fish'); مراست again states 'are mine' (مرا + است). The list affirms that these auspicious signs/things belong to him.
segments.s761-g17: دلت = دل + enclitic ت, so 'دلت' is 'your heart.' را is the definite/object marker placed after the object (standard Persian word order: object + را before the verb). به تیمار چندین is a prepositional phrase ('to/onto care(s) — many cares'), with چندین (an indefinite distributive) modifying تیمار. مبند is the negative imperative (م‑ + present stem بند from بستن) meaning 'do not bind/attach.' The whole segment literally: 'Do not bind your heart to many cares.'
segments.s761-g18: بس here works as an adverb/intensifier ('enough' / 'too much' — i.e. 'do not be overly...'). ایمن is an adjective 'safe, secure.' مشو is the negative imperative/negative present of شدن (مَشَو) = 'do not become.' بر سپهر بلند is a locative/adverbial phrase ('upon the lofty firmament/heavens'); adjective بلند follows the noun as in poetry. The clause warns against overconfidence in exalted position.
segments.s761-g19: که introduces a relative/subordinate clause ('who/that'). با ... و با ... repeats the preposition for emphasis: 'with elephants and with lion‑taming' (i.e. keeps/uses elephants and lion‑tamers/performers). کند is the 3rd person singular present/habitual 'does' — here describing what such a man does or has.
segments.s761-g20: چنان دان is an imperative/hortative 'consider/know thus' (دان is an archaic/imperative form of دانستن used to mean 'consider/know'). که introduces the content clause: 'that ...'. از + بی‌نیازی = 'from/out of self‑sufficiency' (بی‌نیازی = self‑sufficiency / indifference). کند repeats the 3rd‑person present 'does' and links back to the action: 'he does it out of self‑sufficiency.'
segments.s761-g21: تو is the 2nd‑person pronoun. بیجان is a predicative adjective 'lifeless/dead.' شوی is the 2nd‑person singular present/subjunctive of شدن ('you become' / in prophetic/narrative register 'you will become'). او is the 3rd‑person pronoun; بماند is 3rd‑person present/subjunctive of ماندن ('he remains' / 'will remain'). دراز here qualifies بماند ('remain long'). The pair of clauses contrast the addressee's fate with another's endurance.
segments.s761-g22: درازست is the contracted copular phrase دراز است ('is long'), with the predicate preceding the subject by poetic inversion. گفتار is the noun 'speech/utterance' (the subject). چندین modifies مناز ('many dwellings/stations'); grammatically this is a noun phrase following the copula, so the literal reading is 'Speech is long; many dwellings.' (In context this gestures to a lengthy discourse and plural stations/abodes).
segments.s761-g23: تو 'you' is the subject. از introduces the standard of comparison ('than' in comparative constructions). آفریدون is the comparator (the legendary king). فزونتر is the comparative adjective 'more/greater' (فزون + تر). نه‌ای is the negative copula (second‑person 'you are not') — the clause asserts you are not superior to Afridun.
segments.s761-g24: چو means 'like/as.' پرویز is the name used as a model. باتخت is با + تخت 'with throne' (a prepositional/adverbial phrase meaning 'in throne'); و links the next item; افسر (literally 'helmet') functions here as 'diadem/crown' (a poetic usage). نه‌ای again is the negative copula 'you are not.' The clause says you are not like Parviz in possessing throne and crown.

sentences.s761-s21: In this way, until he had reached sixteen years of age, the moon and the sun kept passing over his head.
sentences.s761-s17: I grant greatness to whoever was the lesser; I do not afflict that one who was the greater.
sentences.s761-s18: I do not seek greatness and wisdom; I seek instead battle, fierceness, and manliness.
sentences.s761-s19: That no one's strength and fortune remain; neither treasure nor rural kingship nor throne.
sentences.s761-s20: One must have an eternal name, not desire; cast away desire and exalt the name.
sentences.s761-s1: When he passed away, Yazdgerd became king, in the month Spandarmad on the day Ord.
sentences.s761-s2: What did that eloquent, brave man say, when he returned, sated, from the day's circuit?
sentences.s761-s3: That once my mother did (not) bear me — the lofty heaven did not turn away from me.
sentences.s761-s4: Within a tight compass, between two spheres, what can I say? There is nothing but silence.
sentences.s761-s5: Neither a day of greatness nor a day of need remains long for anyone.
sentences.s761-s6: The times are not with us; when you look, no one has the means of judgment.
sentences.s761-s7: For the strength at the table and for the measure of the cup, do not let worldly care take away any name (reputation).
sentences.s761-s8: If the revolving wheel (fate) drags you from this saddle, in the end ashes will be your pillow.
sentences.s761-s13: Look closely at what this proud Haft-gerd did to Yazdegerd.
sentences.s761-s14: When he sat down in the royal seat, joyful, he placed a large hat upon his head.
sentences.s761-s15: Thus he said: 'From the distant turning wheel of heaven I am a pure offspring of sweet‑souled stock.'
sentences.s761-s16: From father to father the kingship is mine; the sun and the ear (of grain) and the sign of the Fish are mine.
sentences.s761-s9: Do not bind your heart to many cares; do not become too secure on the lofty heavens.
sentences.s761-s10: Know this: he who keeps elephants and lion‑taming (people) does so; consider that he does it out of self‑sufficiency.
sentences.s761-s11: You will become lifeless; he will remain a long time. My speech is long — (do) not (seek) many dwellings.
sentences.s761-s12: You are not greater than Afridun; you are not, like Parviz, with throne and diadem.
